name: Dhb's Spaghetti Sauce
recipeIngredient: 75 pounds ripe Creole tomatoes or Roma tomatoes
recipeInstructions:
Do not substitute curly parsley.
Use good-quality olive oil.
1. Wash and core the tomatoes. Cut them into quarters or sixths.
2. Place the tomatoes in a large, non-aluminum pot (wide is preferable). Bring to a boil, stirring frequently to prevent the bottom tomatoes from burning.
3. Smash the tomatoes as you go.
4. Simmer for about 15 minutes. Remove seeds and skin using a food mill; this will yield approximately 28 quarts.
5. Return liquid to the stove and sauté onion in olive oil.
6. Add green pepper, garlic, and mushrooms and cook slowly until they begin to caramelize.
7. Stir frequently to prevent settling and burning of the pulp.
8. Add to simmering tomato juice.
9. After about an hour, add basil and parsley.
10. Cook until reduced by half, approximately two hours (longer if desired).
11. Pack into half-pint jars and process in boiling water for 20 minutes.
12. Makes 60 servings of spaghetti sauce.
